steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence lists several elements of Celtic art after ‘These included.’ Because the items are complex, the blank needs punctuation that keeps the series clear.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘significance. Depictions’: A period ends the sentence here, breaking apart a list that should remain connected to ‘These included.’ ✗
  • Choice B — ‘significance: depictions’: A colon introduces something that explains or lists what came before it, but the sentence already presents the full list after ‘These included.’ A colon would make the structure confusing. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘significance; depictions’: The semicolon correctly separates three included elements in a complex series: intricate patterns that often held symbolic significance; depictions of birds, horses, and other animals; and inlaid enamel accents. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘significance, depictions’: A comma alone cannot clearly separate major list elements when one element itself contains commas (‘birds, horses, and other animals’). ✗
That confirms C is correct.
